response.justification: Candidate is a strong generalist software engineer with deep experience in web, backend, and some embedded-adjacent work, but the role requires native-level Japanese (not listed), 3+ years of C for UNIX embedded development (only a brief stint at NI LabWindows in 2014-2015), and specific expertise in LAN-switch security technologies like MACsec, 802.1X, and Linux kernel modification — none of which appear on the resume. The mismatch on hard requirements (language and embedded C/kernel experience) makes this a no.
strengths: Long track record as a senior/staff engineer with strong software craftsmanship and TDD discipline
Some C/C++ experience at National Instruments on developer tooling for engineers
Linux familiarity and DevOps/CI background useful for embedded build environments
concerns: No evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (hard requirement)
Lacks embedded networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch chips)
No demonstrated Linux kernel source modification or chip-vendor SDK experience

key_factors.factor: General senior engineering and Linux background
